config.seed_text: The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.

model.notes: Direct continuation of the current repo's GPT-2 lineage.

records.reflection_text: The only reason he was able to learn it is because of this "propagation" with which his own life has been built for many centuries (his writing, as an interpreter) during such times. I will elaborate upon some other ways written words can be changed by your computer software program from my experience while playing Wargaming against these people – there may even still exist one known exception at every turn if you ever have any intention or need thereof! So let me now introduce our new Librarians' Manual , where we look back over each major piece introduced into today's book review article…
records.output_text:
I'm going through all five editions here together so no more repeating myself 🙂 And please share everything about us using whatever tool makes sense; thanks again everybody!! We'll see ya later….

 [Back To Top]
records.source_window_text: The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.
records.reflection_window_text: The only reason he was able to learn it is because of this "propagation" with which his own life has been built for many centuries (his writing, as an interpreter) during such times. I will elaborate upon some other ways written words can be changed by your computer software program from my experience while playing Wargaming against these people – there may even still exist one known exception at every turn if you ever have any intention or need thereof! So let me now introduce our new Librarians' Manual , where we look back over each major piece introduced into today's book review article…

reflection_generation.prompt:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Analyze the following text and do two things:
1. State its strongest recurring ideas in 2-4 short bullet points.
2. Produce a revised continuation that makes those ideas more structurally coherent and more surprising.

TEXT:
The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.
reflection_generation.full_text:
You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Analyze the following text and do two things:
1. State its strongest recurring ideas in 2-4 short bullet points.
2. Produce a revised continuation that makes those ideas more structurally coherent and more surprising.

TEXT:
The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions. The only reason he was able to learn it is because of this "propagation" with which his own life has been built for many centuries (his writing, as an interpreter) during such times. I will elaborate upon some other ways written words can be changed by your computer software program from my experience while playing Wargaming against these people – there may even still exist one known exception at every turn if you ever have any intention or need thereof! So let me now introduce our new Librarians' Manual , where we look back over each major piece introduced into today's book review article…
reflection_generation.continuation_text: The only reason he was able to learn it is because of this "propagation" with which his own life has been built for many centuries (his writing, as an interpreter) during such times. I will elaborate upon some other ways written words can be changed by your computer software program from my experience while playing Wargaming against these people – there may even still exist one known exception at every turn if you ever have any intention or need thereof! So let me now introduce our new Librarians' Manual , where we look back over each major piece introduced into today's book review article…

REFLECTION:
The only reason he was able to learn it is because of this "propagation" with which his own life has been built for many centuries (his writing, as an interpreter) during such times. I will elaborate upon some other ways written words can be changed by your computer software program from my experience while playing Wargaming against these people – there may even still exist one known exception at every turn if you ever have any intention or need thereof! So let me now introduce our new Librarians' Manual , where we look back over each major piece introduced into today's book review article…

You are a text-only language model participating in a controlled recursion experiment.

Use the reflection below to write the next evolved version of the text. Keep it as connected prose, not bullet points.

ORIGINAL TEXT:
The last librarian trained a language model on a dead civilization's poetry and discovered that its footnotes were predictions.

REFLECTION:
The only reason he was able to learn it is because of this "propagation" with which his own life has been built for many centuries (his writing, as an interpreter) during such times. I will elaborate upon some other ways written words can be changed by your computer software program from my experience while playing Wargaming against these people – there may even still exist one known exception at every turn if you ever have any intention or need thereof! So let me now introduce our new Librarians' Manual , where we look back over each major piece introduced into today's book review article…I'm going through all five editions here together so no more repeating myself 🙂 And please share everything about us using whatever tool makes sense; thanks again everybody!! We'll see ya later….
